text: Hello and welcome to Real Easy English.
zh: 大家好,欢迎收听《Real Easy English》。

text: In this podcast, we have real conversations in Easy English to help you learn.
zh: 在这档播客节目中,我们用简单英语进行真实对话,帮助大家学习英语。

text: I'm Neil.
zh: 我是尼尔。

text: And I'm Georgie.
zh: 我是乔吉。

text: You can watch a video version of this podcast and find a free worksheet to help you learn.
zh: 你可以观看这档播客的视频版本,还能找到免费的学习讲义来帮助你学习。

text: Go to bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 请访问 bbclearningenglish.com。

text: Hi, Georgie.
zh: 嗨,乔吉。

text: You're looking very summery today.
zh: 你今天看起来很有夏日气息啊。

text: Yes, well that is because I'm going on my summer holiday tomorrow.
zh: 是啊,因为我明天就要去度暑假了。

text: Well, that's exciting and very useful because we're talking about summer plans today.
zh: 那真令人兴奋,而且正好合适,因为我们今天要聊的就是暑假计划。

text: It's summer in the UK at the moment, which is a time when people are often busy.
zh: 现在英国正值夏天,这段时间人们通常都很忙。

text: So, Georgie, you're going on holiday.
zh: 那么,乔吉,你要去度假了。

text: Tell me more about that.
zh: 跟我多说说吧。

text: So, I'm at an age now where lots of my friends are getting married, married.
zh: 我现在到了这个年纪,身边很多朋友都在结婚。

text: So I have a few weddings this year.
zh: 所以我今年要参加好几场婚礼。

text: But something that's quite traditional before a wedding is for the bride or the groom to have like a celebration.
zh: 不过婚礼前有个挺传统的习俗,就是新娘或新郎要办一场庆祝活动。

text: This could be a party or more recently, it's become quite common to have a weekend away with a lot of their friends.
zh: 这可能是一个派对,不过最近更常见的做法是和一大群朋友一起去外地过一个周末。

text: So that's what I'm doing this weekend.
zh: 这周末我就要去做这件事。

text: And that's what is called a hen party for for a woman or a stag party for a man in the UK.
zh: 在英国,这种活动女方叫“hen party”(单身女子派对),男方叫“stag party”(单身汉派对)。

text: I think in the US it's different.
zh: 我记得在美国说法不太一样。

text: Hmm, a bachelor party.
zh: 嗯,是叫“bachelor party”(单身汉派对)。

text: Yes.
zh: 没错。

text: What about you, Neil?
zh: 你呢,尼尔?

text: What are you up to this summer?
zh: 你今年夏天打算做什么?

text: Well, we're not going away.
zh: 嗯,我们不出远门。

text: We're staying in the UK, but that's okay for me because the weather is actually nice in the summer here, usually.
zh: 我们会留在英国,不过这对我来说也挺好的,因为这里夏天的天气其实通常都不错。

text: So we might do some day trips, go to the beach, maybe a bit of camping with the kids, and then go on our proper holiday in October.
zh: 所以我们可能会去做一些一日游,去海边,也许带孩子去露营一下,然后十月份再去一次真正的度假。

text: That sounds nice.
zh: 听起来不错。

text: So if you're not going on holiday, do you have any goals, like things you want to complete or get done during the summer?
zh: 那如果你不去度假,你有没有一些目标,比如今年夏天想完成或做好的事情?

text: Well, I have a garden and it always needs things doing to it.
zh: 嗯,我家有个花园,总有些事情要打理。

text: But actually, the only thing I really do is mow the lawn, cut the grass using that machine called a lawnmower.
zh: 不过说实话,我唯一真正会做的就是修剪草坪,用那种叫割草机的机器把草剪短。

text: I seem to do that a lot in the summer.
zh: 我夏天好像总是在干这个。

text: How about you, Georgie?
zh: 你呢,乔吉?

text: Have you got any goals or plans, things you want to do over the summer?
zh: 你有没有什么目标或计划,想在夏天做的事情?

text: So I don't have a garden at my flat, but we do have a roof terrace.
zh: 我公寓里没有花园,不过我们有个屋顶露台。

text: And at the moment, it looks pretty disgusting.
zh: 目前它看起来相当糟糕。

text: It's not in a very good state.
zh: 状态不太好。

text: So my housemates and I want to to clean that up a little bit.
zh: 所以我和室友们想把它稍微收拾一下。

text: And we're also planning to buy some outdoor furniture, maybe even a barbecue.
zh: 我们还打算买一些户外家具,甚至可能买个烧烤架。

text: Wow, that sounds good.
zh: 哇,听起来不错。

text: Do you like DIY when you have to build and make things?
zh: 你喜欢自己动手做东西吗,就是那种DIY?

text: It's not my favourite thing, to be honest.
zh: 说实话,这不是我最喜欢的事情。

text: I think I'm a bit too lazy for it.
zh: 我觉得自己有点太懒了。

text: But if there's a goal and something nice that we'll have at the end, then I'll do it.
zh: 不过如果有个目标,最后能得到一些不错的成果,那我还是愿意去做的。

text: Why not?
zh: 何乐而不为呢。

text: I love to barbecue in the summer.
zh: 我夏天很喜欢烧烤。

text: Do you?
zh: 是吗?

text: Yeah.
zh: 是啊。

text: What do you like to put on the barbecue?
zh: 你喜欢在烤架上放什么?

text: Well, all kinds of things.
zh: 嗯,各种各样的东西都有。

text: So people often think about burgers and sausages, but I like to do some other things, quite a lot of fish.
zh: 大家通常会想到汉堡和香肠,不过我还喜欢做点别的,比如挺多的鱼。

text: I like to grill fish on the barbecue.
zh: 我喜欢在烧烤架上烤鱼。

text: Oh, you're making me hungry.
zh: 哦,你说得我都饿了。

text: It's nearly lunchtime.
zh: 都快到午饭时间了。

text: Yeah.
zh: 是啊。

text: And something that you can't do in the UK in the winter, which you can do in the summer, which I love, is stay out late in the evening because it's light.
zh: 还有一点我很喜欢,就是在英国冬天做不到、但夏天可以做的事——晚上可以在外面待到很晚,因为天还亮着。

text: Right now, it's light until almost 10 o 'clock at night.
zh: 现在天几乎要到晚上十点才黑。

text: So I like barbecuing in the summer.
zh: 所以我夏天很喜欢烧烤。

text: Georgie, is there anything that you do or like doing in the summer that you can't do other times of year?
zh: 乔吉,你有没有什么事情是只在夏天做、或者特别喜欢在夏天做,而其他季节做不到的?

text: Hmm, good question.
zh: 嗯,好问题。

text: Yes, I really like going on picnics with friends and I actually did that last week with some friends.
zh: 有的,我特别喜欢和朋友们去野餐,上周我其实就和朋友们去野餐了。

text: So you just buy lots of small snacks from the shop or you can cook stuff, I guess, and bring it.
zh: 你可以从商店买很多小零食,或者自己做点东西带过去。

text: You find a park and then sit and eat with friends, maybe play some music.
zh: 找个公园,然后坐下和朋友们一起吃东西,也许还放点音乐。

text: Let's recap the vocabulary we heard in this conversation, starting with the question, what are you up to?
zh: 让我们来回顾一下这段对话中出现的词汇,先从这个问题开始:“what are you up to?”

text: Which means, what are you doing?
zh: 意思是“你在做什么?”

text: What are you up to tomorrow, Georgie?
zh: 乔吉,你明天打算做什么(What are you up to tomorrow)?

text: I am going to the airport tomorrow.
zh: 我明天要去机场。

text: We've also had DIY, which is short for do it yourself, and it means tasks around the house like painting or building furniture.
zh: 我们还提到了“DIY”,这是“do it yourself”(自己动手做)的缩写,指的是家里的一些活儿,比如刷漆或组装家具。

text: For example, I'm going to do some DIY on my roof terrace this summer.
zh: 比如说,我今年夏天打算在屋顶露台上做点DIY。

text: We've heard barbecue.
zh: 我们还听到了“barbecue”(烧烤)。

text: Now, a barbecue is an outdoor grill for cooking on, but it's also the name of the meal that you cook on the barbecue or the event or party that you have with barbecued food.
zh: “barbecue”指的是用来烹饪的户外烤架,不过它也可以指用烤架做出来的那顿饭,或者指你举办的那种以烧烤食物为主的聚会或活动。

text: A picnic is when you take food to an outdoor space like a park or the beach.
zh: “picnic”(野餐)是指把食物带到户外场所,比如公园或海滩去吃。

text: You usually sit on a blanket and eat on the ground.
zh: 通常你会坐在野餐垫上,在地上吃东西。

text: And we heard mow the lawn, and that means cutting the grass with a lawnmower.
zh: 我们还听到了“mow the lawn”(修剪草坪),意思是用割草机剪草。

text: And that's it for this episode of Real Easy English.
zh: 这就是本期《Real Easy English》的全部内容。

text: Now, test the vocabulary you've learnt using the worksheet on our website.
zh: 现在,可以用我们网站上的学习讲义来测试一下你学到的词汇。

text: Find it in the links below this episode or visit bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 可以在本集下方的链接中找到,或者访问 bbclearningenglish.com。

text: Goodbye!
zh: 再见!
